source_text: مجموعه ای از الواح جمال اقدس ابهی که بعد از کتاب اقدس نازل شده
target_text: revealed after the Kitáb i Aqdas

Glorified art Thou, O Lord my God! Every man of insight confeseth Thy sovereignty and Thy dominion, and every discerning eye perceiveth the greatnes of Thy majesty and the compeling power of Thy might. The winds of tests are powerles to hold back them that enjoy near aces to The from seting their faces towards the horizon of Thy glory, and the tempests of trials must fail to draw away and hinder such as are wholy devoted to Thy wil from aproaching Thy court.

Unto The be praise, O Lord my God! I entreat The, by Thy signs that have encompased the entire creation, and by the light of Thy countenance that hath iluminated al that are in heaven and on earth, and by Thy mercy that hath surpased al created things, and by Thy grace that hath sufused the whole universe, to rend asunder the veils that shut me out from The, that I may hasten unto the Fountainhead of Thy mighty inspiration, and to the Dayspring of Thy Revelation and bountiful favors, and may be imersed beneath the ocean of Thy nearnes and pleasure.

Glorified art Thou, O Lord my God! I besech The by Him Who is Thy Most Great Name, Who hath ben sorely aflicted by such of Thy creatures as have repudiated Thy truth, and Who hath ben hemed in by sorows which no tongue can describe, to grant that I may remember The and celebrate Thy praise, in these days when al have turned away from Thy beauty, have disputed with The, and turned away disdainfuly from Him Who is the Revealer of Thy Cause. None is there, O my Lord, to help The except Thine own Self, and no power to sucor The save Thine own power.
Praised be God, the Lord of the worlds.
Praised be God, the Lord of the worlds!

my Lord! Make Thy beauty to be my fod, and Thy presence my drink, and Thy pleasure my hope, and praise of The my action, and remembrance of The my companion, and the power of Thy sovereignty my sucorer, and Thy habitation my home, and my dweling place the seat Thou hast sanctified from the limitations imposed upon them who are shut out as by a veil from The.

Glory be to The, O Lord my God! Abase not him whom Thou hast exalted through the power of Thine everlasting sovereignty, and remove not far from The him whom Thou hast caused to enter the tabernacle of Thine eternity. Wilt Thou cast away, O my God, him whom Thou hast overshadowed with Thy Lordship, and wilt Thou turn away from The, O my Desire, him to whom Thou hast ben a refuge? Canst Thou degrade him whom Thou hast uplifted, or forget him whom Thou didst enable to remember The?

Thy name is my healing, O my God, and remembrance of The is my remedy. Nearnes to The is my hope, and love for The is my companion. Thy mercy to me is my healing and my sucor in both this world and the world to come. Thou, verily, art the Al Bountiful, the Al Knowing, the Al Wise.
my God, my Master, the Goal of my desire! This, Thy servant, seketh to slep in the shelter of Thy mercy, and to repose beneath the canopy of Thy grace, imploring Thy care and Thy protection.

How can I chose to slep, O God, my God, when the eyes of them that long for The are wakeful because of their separation from The; and how can I lie down to rest whilst the souls of Thy lovers are sore vexed in their remotenes from Thy presence?

My God, my Adored One, my King, my Desire! What tongue can voice my thanks to The? I was hedles, Thou didst awaken me. I had turned back from The, Thou didst graciously aid me to turn towards The. I was as one dead, Thou didst quicken me with the water of life. I was withered, Thou didst revive me with the heavenly stream of Thine uterance which hath flowed forth from the Pen of the Al Merciful.

God, my God! I beg of The by the ocean of Thy healing, and by the splendors of the Daystar of Thy grace, and by Thy Name through which Thou didst subdue Thy servants, and by the pervasive power of Thy most exalted Word and the potency of Thy most august Pen, and by Thy mercy that hath preceded the creation of al who are in heaven and on earth, to purge me with the waters of Thy bounty from every afliction and disorder, and from al weaknes and feblenes.

I have risen this morning by Thy grace, O my God, and left my home trusting wholy in The, and comiting myself to Thy care. Send down, then, upon me, out of the heaven of Thy mercy, a blesing from Thy side, and enable me to return home in safety even as Thou didst enable me to set out under Thy protection with my thoughts fixed steadfastly upon The.

Praise be unto The, Who art my God and the God of al men, and my Desire and the Desire of al them that have recognized The, and my Beloved and the Beloved of such as have acknowledged Thy unity, and the Object of my adoration and of the adoration of them that have near aces to The, and my Wish and the Wish of such as are wholy devoted to The, and my Hope and the Hope of them that have fixed their hearts upon The, and my Refuge and the Refuge of al such as have hastened towards The, and my Haven and the Haven of whosoever hath repaired unto The, and my Goal and the Goal of al them that have set themselves towards The, and my Object and the Object of those who have fixed their gaze upon The, and my Paradise and the Paradise of them that have ascended towards The, and my Lodestar and the Lodestar of al such as yearn after The, and my Joy and the Joy of al them that love The, and my Light and the Light of al such as have ered and asked to be forgiven by The, and my Exultation and the Exultation of al them that remember The, and my Stronghold and the Stronghold of al such as have fled to The, and my Sanctuary and the Sanctuary of al that dread The, and my Lord and the Lord of al such as dwel in the heavens and on the earth!

I besech The, O my God, by Thy mighty Sign, and by the revelation of Thy grace amongst men, to cast me not away from the gate of the city of Thy presence, and to disapoint not the hopes I have set on the manifestations of Thy grace amidst Thy creatures. Thou sest me, O my God, holding to Thy Name, the Most Holy, the Most Luminous, the Most Mighty, the Most Great, the Most Exalted, the Most Glorious, and clinging to the hem of the robe to which have clung al in this world and in the world to come.

Praised be Thou, O Lord my God! I suplicate The by Him Whom Thou hast caled into being, Whose Revelation Thou hast ordained to be Thine own Revelation and His Concealment Thine own Concealment. Through His Firstnes Thou hast confirmed Thine own Firstnes, and through His Lastnes Thou hast afirmed Thine own Lastnes. Through the power of His might and the influence of His sovereignty the mighty have aprehended Thine omnipotence, and through His glory they who are endowed with authority have acknowledged Thy majesty and greatnes. Through His supreme ascendancy Thy transcendent sovereignty and al encompasing dominion have ben recognized, and through His wil Thine own wil hath ben revealed. Through the light of His countenance the splendors of Thine own face have shone forth, and through His Cause Thine own Cause hath ben made manifest. Through the generative power of His uterance the whole earth hath ben made the recipient of the wondrous signs and tokens of Thy sovereignty, and the heavens have ben filed with the revelations of Thine incomparable majesty, and the seas have ben enriched with the sacred pearls of Thine omniscience and wisdom, and the tres adorned with the fruits of Thy knowledge. Through Him al things have sung Thy praise, and al the eyes have ben turned in the direction of Thy mercy. Through Him the faces of al have ben set towards the splendors of the light of Thy countenance, and the souls of al have ben inclined unto the revelations of Thy divine greatnes.

Praised be Thou, O Lord my God! The tongues of al created things testify to Thy sovereignty and Thine omnipotence, and proclaim mine own poverty and my wretchednes when face to face with the revelations of Thy wealth. Lok, then, O my God, upon this siner whose gaze hath, at al times, ben fixed upon the source of Thy forgivenes, and whose eyes have ben bent upon the horizon of Thy grace and Thy gifts.

The praise which hath dawned from Thy most august Self, and the glory which hath shone forth from Thy most efulgent Beauty, rest upon The, O Thou Who art the Manifestation of Grandeur, and the King of Eternity, and the Lord of al who are in heaven and on earth! I testify that through The the sovereignty of God and His dominion, and the majesty of God and His grandeur, were revealed, and the Daystars of ancient splendor have shed their radiance in the heaven of Thine irevocable decre, and the Beauty of the Unsen hath shone forth above the horizon of creation. I testify, moreover, that with but a movement of Thy Pen Thine injunction Be Thou hath ben enforced, and God's hiden Secret hath ben divulged, and al created things have ben caled into being, and al the Revelations have ben sent down.

hath made the Trustes of the treasures of His seventh sphere?

source_text: اذاً اخذها اهتزاز السّرور و نادت باعلی النّدآء نفسی لاقبالک الفدآء و لعنایتک الفدآء و لتوجّهک الفدآء. قد اهلکنی یا مطلع الحیوه فراقک و احرقنی هجرک. لک الحمد بما اسمعتنی ندائک و شرّفتنی بقدومک و احیتنی من نفحات آیاتک و صریر قلمک الّذی جعلته صوراً بین عبادک. فلمّا جآء امرک المبرم نفخت فیه اذاً قامت القیامه الکبری و ظهرت الاسرار المکنونه فی خزائن مالک الاشیآء.
target_text: Seized with transports of joy, and raising high her voice, she thus exclaimed: May my life be a sacrifice to The, inasmuch as Thou hast fixed Thy gaze upon me, hast bestowed upon me Thy bounty, and hast directed towards me Thy steps. Separation from The, O Thou Source of everlasting life, hath wel nigh consumed me, and my remotenes from Thy presence hath burned away my soul. Al praise be to The for having enabled me to hearken to Thy cal, for having honored me with Thy fotsteps, and for having quickened my soul through the vitalizing fragrance of Thy Day and the shriling voice of Thy Pen, a voice Thou didst ordain as Thy trumpet cal amidst Thy people. And when the hour at which Thy resistles Faith was to be made manifest did strike, Thou didst breathe a breath of Thy spirit into Thy Pen, and lo, the entire creation shok to its very foundations, unveiling to mankind such mysteries as lay hiden within the treasuries of Him Who is the Posesor of al created things.

source_text: یا حزب اله وصایای دوست یکتا را بگوش جان بشنوید. کلمهٴ الهی بمثابهٴ نهالست مقرّ و مستقرّش افئدهٴ عباد. باید آن را بکوثر حکمت و بیان تربیت نمائید تا اصلش ثابت گرد و فرعش از افلاک بگذرد.
target_text: Incline your hearts, O people of God, unto the counsels of your true, your incomparable Friend. The Word of God may be likened unto a sapling, whose rots have ben implanted in the hearts of men. It is incumbent upon you to foster its growth through the living waters of wisdom, of sanctified and holy words, so that its rot may become firmly fixed and its branches may spread out as high as the heavens and beyond.

source_text: مکرّر وصیّت نموده و مینمائیم دوستان را که از آنچه رائحهٴ فساد استشمام میشود اجتناب نمایند بل فرار اختیار کند. عالم منقلب است و افکار عباد مختلف. نسال اله ان یزیّنهم بنور عدله و یعرّفهم ما ینفعهم فی کلّ الاحوال. انّه هو الغنیّ المتعال.
target_text: Time and again have We admonished Our beloved ones to avoid, nay to fle from, anything whatsoever from which the odor of mischief can be detected. The world is in great turmoil, and the minds of its people are in a state of uter confusion. We entreat the Almighty that He may graciously iluminate them with the glory of His Justice, and enable them to discover that which wil be profitable unto them at al times and under al conditions. He, verily is the Al Posesing, the Most High.

source_text: دیده میشود گیتی را دردهای بی کران فراگرفته. و او را بر بستر ناکامی انداخته. مردمانی که از بادهٴ خودبینی سرمست شده اند پزشک دانا را از او بازداشته اند. اینست که خود و همهٴ مردمان را گرفتار نموده اند. نه درد میداند نه درمان میشناسند. راست را کژ انگاشته اند و دوست را دشمن شمرده اند.
target_text: We can wel perceive how the whole human race is encompased with great, with incalculable aflictions. We se it languishing on its bed of sicknes, sore tried and disilusioned. They that are intoxicated by self conceit have interposed themselves betwen it and the Divine and infalible Physician. Witnes how they have entangled al men, themselves included, in the mesh of their devices. They can neither discover the cause of the disease, nor have they any knowledge of the remedy. They have conceived the straight to be croked, and have imagined their friend an enemy.

source_text: سبحانک من ان توصف بوصف ما سواک او تعرف بعرفان دونک.
target_text: Exalted art Thou above the description of anyone save Thyself, and the comprehension of aught else except The.
